18	// loadExecutionsMirrorConfig reads .ddx/config.yaml at projectRoot and returns
19	// the executions.mirror block when configured. Errors return nil silently —

22	func loadExecutionsMirrorConfig(projectRoot string) *config.ExecutionsMirrorConfig {

39	func (f *CommandFactory) newAgentExecuteBeadCommand() *cobra.Command {
40		cmd := &cobra.Command{
41			Use:   "execute-bead <bead-id>",
42			Short: "Run an agent on one bead in an isolated worktree, then land the result",
43			Long: `execute-bead is the primitive: it runs a single agent on a single bead in
44	an isolated git worktree (the worker step), then hands the result to the
45	parent-side orchestrator which merges or preserves the commits.
46	
47	The worker classifies its outcome as one of:
48	  task_succeeded  — agent exited 0 and produced commits
49	  task_no_changes — agent exited 0 but made no commits
50	  task_failed     — agent exited non-zero
51	
52	The orchestrator then applies landing logic (merge, gate evaluation, preserve)
53	and the final result reflects both the worker and landing decisions.
54	
55	For normal queue-driven work use "ddx agent execute-loop", which claims
56	ready beads, calls this command, and closes or unclaims each bead based on
57	the result. Reach for execute-bead directly only to debug or re-run a
58	specific bead.
59	
60	Result status is reported on the "status:" line and is one of:
61	  success                          — merged (or preserved with --no-merge)
62	  no_changes                       — agent ran but produced no diff
63	  already_satisfied                — closed by the loop after repeated no_changes
64	  land_conflict                    — merge failed; result preserved
65	  post_run_check_failed            — checks failed; result preserved
66	  execution_failed                 — agent or harness error
67	  structural_validation_failed     — bead or prompt inputs invalid
68	
69	execute-loop closes the bead with session/commit evidence only on success
70	(and already_satisfied); every other status leaves the bead open and
71	unclaimed for a later attempt.`,
72			Example: `  # Debug one specific bead (prefer "execute-loop" for normal queue work)
73	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c
74	
75	  # Run against a non-HEAD base revision
76	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --from main
77	
78	  # Preserve the result under refs/ddx/iterations/... instead of merging
79	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --no-merge
80	
81	  # Pin harness/model for a debugging pass
82	  ddx agent execute-bead ddx-7a01ba6c --harness codex`,
83			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
84			RunE: f.runAgentExecuteBead,
85		}
86		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Project root path (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
87		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
88		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-merge", false, "Skip merge; preserve result under refs/ddx/iterations/<bead-id>/<timestamp>-<base-shortsha> instead")
89		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness to use")
90		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model override")
91		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider name (e.g. vidar, openrouter); selects a named provider from config")
92		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
93		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
94		cmd.Flags().String("context-budget", "", "Context budget for prompt: empty (full), minimal (omit large governing docs for cheap-tier)")
95		cmd.Flags().String("prompt", "", "Prompt file path (auto-generated from bead if omitted)")
96		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output result as JSON")
97		return cmd
98	}

100	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteBead(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
101		beadID := args[0]
102		if !validBeadID.MatchString(beadID) {
103			return fmt.Errorf("invalid bead ID %q: must contain only letters, digits, dots, underscores, and hyphens", beadID)
104		}
105		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
106		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
107		noMerge, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-merge")
108		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
109		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
110		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
111		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
112		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
113		contextBudget, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("context-budget")
114		promptFile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("prompt")
115		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
116	
117		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
118	
119		rcfg, err := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, config.CLIOverrides{
120			Harness:       harness,
121			Model:         model,
122			Provider:      provider,
123			ModelRef:      modelRef,
124			Effort:        effort,
125			ContextBudget: contextBudget,
126		})
127		if err != nil {
128			return fmt.Errorf("load config at %s: %w", projectRoot, err)
129		}
130		runtime := agent.ExecuteBeadRuntime{
131			FromRev:    fromRev,
132			PromptFile: promptFile,
133			WorkerID:   os.Getenv("DDX_WORKER_ID"),
134			BeadEvents: bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx")),
135		}
136	
137		var gitOps agent.GitOps = &agent.RealGitOps{}
138		if f.executeBeadGitOverride != nil {
139			gitOps = f.executeBeadGitOverride
140		}
141	
142		var orchestratorGitOps agent.OrchestratorGitOps = &agent.RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
143		if f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride != nil {
144			orchestratorGitOps = f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride
145		}
146	
147		// Test injection seam: AgentRunnerOverride feeds canned *Result values
148		// from fakes without spinning up a real provider. Production callers
149		// leave this nil so ExecuteBead constructs an agent service from
150		// projectRoot internally.
151		if f.AgentRunnerOverride != nil {
152			runtime.AgentRunner = f.AgentRunnerOverride
153		}
154	
155		// Preflight the orphan-model check before creating a worktree. Mirrors
156		// the execute-loop preflight so `ddx agent execute-bead --model <unroutable>`
157		// errors before any git work happens rather than mid-execution. Skip the
158		// preflight when a test runner override is in use — overrides may be
159		// fakes that bypass routing entirely.
160		if f.AgentRunnerOverride == nil {
161			if err := agent.ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService(cmd.Context(), f.WorkingDir, harness, model, provider, modelRef); err != nil {
162				return err
163			}
164		}
165	
166		// Recover any orphaned worktrees from previous crashed runs before
167		// spawning a new worker. This is the parent's responsibility.
168		agent.RecoverOrphans(gitOps, projectRoot, beadID)
169	
170		// Worker step: run the agent in an isolated worktree.
171		res, err := agent.ExecuteBeadWithConfig(cmd.Context(), projectRoot, beadID, rcfg, runtime, gitOps)
172		if err != nil && res == nil {
173			return err
174		}
175	
176		// Orchestrator step: land the result (ff or merge → push, gate eval,
177		// preserve). Always run when we have a result — the orchestrator handles
178		// all cases including no-changes and error (agent failed with no commits).
179		//
180		// Single-bead CLI is strictly serial (no sibling workers in this
181		// process), so we can wire the LandingAdvancer to call Land() directly
182		// without going through a per-project coordinator goroutine. Tests may
183		// inject a custom advancer via f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride.
184		if res != nil {
185			var advancer func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error)
186			if f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride != nil {
187				advancer = f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride
188			} else {
189				landingGitOps := landingGitOpsFromFactory(f)
190				advancer = func(r *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
191					return agent.Land(projectRoot, agent.BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r), landingGitOps)
192				}
193			}
194			landingOpts := agent.BeadLandingOptions{
195				NoMerge:         noMerge,
196				LandingAdvancer: advancer,
197			}
198	
199			// Wire required execution gates: when the worker manifest declares
200			// governing IDs, gate-eval them in an ephemeral worktree at ResultRev
201			// before LandBeadResult decides merge vs preserve. The original
202			// worker worktree was cleaned up by ExecuteBead, so we pin ResultRev
203			// to a transient ref and check it out into a temp worktree for the
204			// duration of gate evaluation.
205			if res != nil && res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev && res.ExitCode == 0 {
206				wt, ids, cleanup, ctxErr := agent.BuildLandingGateContext(projectRoot, res, gitOps)
207				if ctxErr != nil {
208					// Soft-fail: log and skip gate eval rather than abort the land.
209					fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ddx: warning: gate-context setup failed: %v (skipping required-gate eval)\n", ctxErr)
210				} else if wt != "" {
211					defer cleanup()
212					landingOpts.WtPath = wt
213					landingOpts.GovernIDs = ids
214					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactPath = filepath.Join(projectRoot, res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
215					landingOpts.ChecksArtifactRel = filepath.Join(res.ExecutionDir, "checks.json")
216				}
217			}
218	
219			if landing, landErr := agent.LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchestratorGitOps, landingOpts); landErr == nil {
220				agent.ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
221			} else if err == nil {
222				err = landErr
223			}
224		}
225	
226		if err != nil {
227			_ = writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
228			return err
229		}
230	
231		return writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd, res, asJSON)
232	}
233	
234	func writeExecuteBeadResult(cmd *cobra.Command, res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult, asJSON bool) error {
235		if res == nil {
236			return nil
237		}
238		if asJSON {
239			enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
240			enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
241			return enc.Encode(res)
242		}
243	
244		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "bead:    %s\n", res.BeadID)
245		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "base:    %s\n", res.BaseRev)
246		if res.ResultRev != "" && res.ResultRev != res.BaseRev {
247			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "result:  %s\n", res.ResultRev)
248		}
249		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "outcome: %s\n", res.Outcome)
250		fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "status:  %s\n", res.Status)
251		if res.Detail != "" {
252			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "detail:  %s\n", res.Detail)
253		}
254		if res.NoChangesRationale != "" {
255			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "rationale: %s\n", res.NoChangesRationale)
256		}
257		if res.PreserveRef != "" {
258			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "ref:     %s\n", res.PreserveRef)
259		}
260		return nil
261	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import "strings"
4	
5	// FailureMode constants classify *why* an execution did not land cleanly.
6	// They are orthogonal to Outcome/Status: a single failed attempt carries one
7	// outcome (e.g. preserved) and one failure mode (e.g. merge_conflict) so
8	// measurement surfaces can aggregate failures by cause.
9	const (
10		FailureModeContextOverflow = "context_overflow"
11		FailureModeMergeConflict   = "merge_conflict"
12		FailureModeTestFailure     = "test_failure"
13		FailureModeBuildFailure    = "build_failure"
14		FailureModeTimeout         = "timeout"
15		FailureModeAuthError       = "auth_error"
16		FailureModeNoChanges       = "no_changes"
17		FailureModeRatchetMiss     = "ratchet_miss"
18		FailureModeUnknown         = "unknown"
19	)
20	
21	// ClassifyFailureMode derives a failure_mode for a worker-level result from
22	// its outcome, exit code, and error message. Returns "" when the outcome is
23	// task_succeeded (success carries no failure mode). task_no_changes always
24	// maps to FailureModeNoChanges regardless of error text.
25	//
26	// For task_failed (or non-zero exit code), the error message is scanned for
27	// well-known substrings. The order matters: context/auth/timeout patterns
28	// are checked before generic test/build patterns so a "test timed out"
29	// classifies as timeout rather than test_failure. Anything unrecognised
30	// maps to FailureModeUnknown so aggregates never hide a missed pattern.
31	func ClassifyFailureMode(outcome string, exitCode int, errMsg string) string {
32		switch outcome {
33		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
34			if exitCode == 0 {
35				return ""
36			}
37			// Degenerate: success outcome with non-zero exit. Treat as unknown
38			// failure rather than success so the aggregate flags the anomaly.
39		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
40			return FailureModeNoChanges
41		}
42	
43		lower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)
44		switch {
45		case containsAny(lower,
46			"context length",
47			"context_length_exceeded",
48			"context window",
49			"maximum context",
50			"prompt is too long",
51			"prompt too long",
52			"token limit",
53			"too many tokens",
54			"exceeds the context"):
55			return FailureModeContextOverflow
56		case containsAny(lower,
57			"unauthorized",
58			"401 unauthorized",
59			"invalid api key",
60			"invalid_api_key",
61			"authentication failed",
62			"authentication_error",
63			"no api key",
64			"missing api key",
65			"quota exceeded",
66			"insufficient quota",
67			"insufficient_quota",
68			"rate limit",
69			"rate_limit",
70			"429"):
71			return FailureModeAuthError
72		case containsAny(lower,
73			"timeout",
74			"timed out",
75			"context deadline exceeded",
76			"deadline exceeded",
77			"signal: killed"):
78			return FailureModeTimeout
79		case containsAny(lower,
80			"merge conflict",
81			"ff-merge not possible",
82			"merge failed",
83			"automatic merge failed"):
84			return FailureModeMergeConflict
85		case containsAny(lower,
86			"build failed",
87			"compilation failed",
88			"compile error",
89			"compilation error",
90			"cannot find package",
91			"undefined:",
92			"undefined reference",
93			"syntax error"):
94			return FailureModeBuildFailure
95		case containsAny(lower,
96			"test failed",
97			"tests failed",
98			"--- fail:",
99			"assertion failed",
100			"test failure"):
101			return FailureModeTestFailure
102		}
103		if exitCode != 0 {
104			return FailureModeUnknown
105		}
106		return ""
107	}
108	
109	// containsAny reports whether s contains any of the given substrings. s is
110	// assumed to already be lower-cased by the caller.
111	func containsAny(s string, needles ...string) bool {
112		for _, n := range needles {
113			if n == "" {
114				continue
115			}
116			if strings.Contains(s, n) {
117				return true
118			}
119		}
120		return false
121	}
122	
123	// classifyLandingFailureMode computes the final failure_mode for a result
124	// after the orchestrator has made its landing decision. It prefers
125	// landing-specific signals (merge conflict, gate failure) over the worker's
126	// classification and falls back to workerMode for worker-level failures
127	// (agent execution failed, context overflow, etc.). Returns "" when the
128	// bead merged cleanly.
129	func classifyLandingFailureMode(landingOutcome, landingReason string, gateResults []GateCheckResult, workerMode string) string {
130		switch landingOutcome {
131		case "merged":
132			return ""
133		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
134			return FailureModeNoChanges
135		case "error":
136			if workerMode != "" {
137				return workerMode
138			}
139			return FailureModeUnknown
140		case "preserved":
141			switch landingReason {
142			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
143				return FailureModeMergeConflict
144			case "post-run checks failed":
145				return classifyGateFailure(gateResults)
146			case RatchetPreserveReason:
147				return FailureModeRatchetMiss
148			}
149			// Other preserved reasons (e.g. "agent execution failed",
150			// "--no-merge specified") defer to the worker's classification.
151			if workerMode != "" {
152				return workerMode
153			}
154			return FailureModeUnknown
155		}
156		if workerMode != "" {
157			return workerMode
158		}
159		return FailureModeUnknown
160	}
161	
162	// classifyGateFailure inspects failed gate results to distinguish
163	// build_failure from test_failure. Gate definitions that fail to compile
164	// before running (go build, tsc, cargo build) typically surface build
165	// diagnostics in stderr; test runners surface test failure markers. When
166	// ambiguous, defaults to test_failure since the post-run checks gate is
167	// test-oriented by convention.
168	func classifyGateFailure(gateResults []GateCheckResult) string {
169		for _, gr := range gateResults {
170			if gr.Status != "fail" {
171				continue
172			}
173			combined := strings.ToLower(gr.Stdout + "\n" + gr.Stderr)
174			if containsAny(combined,
175				"build failed",
176				"compilation failed",
177				"compile error",
178				"compilation error",
179				"cannot find package",
180				"undefined:",
181				"undefined reference",
182				"syntax error") {
183				return FailureModeBuildFailure
184			}
185		}
186		return FailureModeTestFailure
187	}
188	
189	// Worker-level outcome constants — set by ExecuteBead() on the result.
190	// The parent orchestrator (LandBeadResult + ApplyLandingToResult) then
191	// overwrites Outcome and Status with the landing decision.
192	const (
193		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded = "task_succeeded"
194		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed    = "task_failed"
195		ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges = "task_no_changes"
196	)
197	
198	// Status constants — used by the loop and other consumers of ExecuteBeadReport.
199	// These map to specific close/unclaim behaviors in ExecuteBeadWorker.
200	const (
201		ExecuteBeadStatusStructuralValidationFailed = "structural_validation_failed"
202		ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed            = "execution_failed"
203		ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed         = "post_run_check_failed"
204		ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed              = "ratchet_failed"
205		ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict               = "land_conflict"
206		ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges                  = "no_changes"
207		ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied           = "already_satisfied"
208		ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess                    = "success"
209	
210		// Post-merge review outcomes. The bead was merged, then reviewed;
211		// the review returned a non-APPROVE verdict and the bead was reopened.
212		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewRequestChanges = "review_request_changes"
213		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewBlock          = "review_block"
214		ExecuteBeadStatusReviewMalfunction    = "review_malfunction"
215	)
216	
217	// ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus maps a landing outcome to the supervisor-visible
218	// status contract. This is called by ApplyLandingToResult and by callers who
219	// build an ExecuteBeadReport from a landing result.
220	func ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus(outcome string, exitCode int, reason string) string {
221		if exitCode != 0 {
222			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
223		}
224	
225		switch outcome {
226		case "merged":
227			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
228		case "no-changes", ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskNoChanges:
229			return ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges
230		case "error":
231			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
232		case "preserved":
233			switch reason {
234			case "merge conflict", "merge failed", "ff-merge not possible":
235				return ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict
236			case "post-run checks failed":
237				return ExecuteBeadStatusPostRunCheckFailed
238			case RatchetPreserveReason:
239				return ExecuteBeadStatusRatchetFailed
240			default:
241				// Preserved iterations may still be success when the caller
242				// explicitly requested no merge.
243				return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
244			}
245		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskSucceeded:
246			return ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess
247		case ExecuteBeadOutcomeTaskFailed:
248			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
249		default:
250			return ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed
251		}
252	}
253	
254	func ExecuteBeadStatusDetail(status, reason, errMsg string) string {
255		switch {
256		case reason != "" && errMsg != "" && reason != errMsg:
257			return reason + ": " + errMsg
258		case reason != "":
259			return reason
260		case errMsg != "":
261			return errMsg
262		case status != "":
263			return status
264		default:
265			return "unknown"
266		}
267	}

data.text:
Committed. The unrelated `TestIntegration_CodexEcho` failure is pre-existing (depends on external codex CLI behavior) and unrelated to this bead.

Summary of changes:
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_status.go` — added `ExecuteBeadStatusPushFailed` constant and `PushFailedReasonPrefix`; `ClassifyExecuteBeadStatus` now maps `merged` + push-failed reason → `push_failed` instead of `success`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — push_failed reports park the bead (365-day cooldown) with `last_status=push_failed` and the push stderr in `last_detail`.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` and `execute_bead_orchestrator.go` — both push-failed reason call sites now use the shared prefix constant.
- `cli/internal/bead/store.go` — `Claim` refuses any bead whose `Extra["execute-loop-last-status"] == "push_failed"`, surfacing the prior push stderr in the error.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_push_failed_test.go` — three tests pin: classification, loop park behavior + event capture, claim-refuses-until-cleared.
